prompt:
Given the case description, identify the MOST relevant CRISPR delivery method from the options below:

a. Plasmid Transfection
b. Lentivirus/Retrovirus
c. RNP/mRNA electroporation
d. RNP/mRNA microinjection
e. mRNA LNP
f. AAV

Category: In vivo
Case Description: I hope to edit Mouse skin

Please provide your response as follows:
- Most relevant method (select one letter a-f):
raw_response:
Thinking and reasoning:
- Goal: perform somatic in vivo editing of mouse skin. Best methods must work in vivo, allow localized delivery to epidermal/dermal cells (many are non-dividing), and be practical in mice.
- Evaluation summary: plasmid transfection is poor in vivo; lentivirus can transduce but integrates and is less commonly preferred for localized somatic skin editing; in vivo RNP/mRNA electroporation is possible but technically demanding; microinjection is for embryos; systemic LNPs favor liver, not skin; AAV is widely used for localized in vivo delivery to mouse skin and transduces non-dividing cells efficiently.
- Conclusion: AAV is the most relevant option among the choices.

Plan:
1. [ ] Identify case specifics and constraints.
2. [ ] Evaluate each delivery option for suitability in mouse skin in vivo.
3. [ ] Select the single most relevant method and present the answer.

Stepwise progress:
1. [✓] Identify case specifics and constraints (completed)
2. [✓] Evaluate each delivery option for suitability (completed)
3. [✓] Select the most relevant method and prepare final answer (completed)

<solution>- Most relevant method (select one letter a-f): f</solution>
